name: Motion (motion.dev)
description: Hardware-accelerated animations using the browser's native Web Animations API and Scroll Timeline — animations run off the main thread, immune to JS jank. 8kb core.
head: <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/motion@12/dist/motion.js"></script>
body:
<script>
(function() {
  var animate = Motion.animate;
  var scroll  = Motion.scroll;
  var inView  = Motion.inView;

  // Animate cards in when they enter view (hardware accelerated)
  inView('.your-card', function(el) {
    animate(el, { opacity: [0, 1], y: [30, 0] }, { duration: 0.6, delay: 0.1, easing: 'ease-out' });
  });

  // Scroll-linked progress bar (runs off main thread)
  var progressBar = document.querySelector('.scroll-progress');
  if (progressBar) {
    scroll(animate(progressBar, { scaleX: [0, 1] }, { easing: 'linear' }));
  }
})();
</script>
